title: Do not default to monochrome brand palettes that eliminate all accent color
summary: Some AI generations default to an all-gray, all-white, all-black minimalist palette that removes accent color entirely, making interfaces feel unfinished or sterile.
instruction: Every interface needs at least one accent color for interactive elements, links, and states. A pure monochrome interface should be an explicit design direction, not the default result of omitting color guidance.
rationale: While overuse of color is a known anti-pattern, complete absence of accent color removes wayfinding cues (links, buttons, interactive elements) and creates a clinical, uninviting interface. Most users need at least one color to orient themselves.
violations: A SaaS dashboard using only black, white, and three shades of gray with no accent color on any interactive element, link, or state.
A marketing page with a black/white/grayscale palette where even buttons and links are gray.
controls: A dashboard using gray as the primary interface color with a single blue accent for links, buttons, and active states.
A marketing page using a warm gray palette with a single brand accent color reserved for CTAs and highlights.
borderlineCases: A photo-centric portfolio where the photography provides all the color and interface elements are intentionally neutral.
contextualExceptions: Photography, art, or design portfolio where the work is the color.
Print-style editorial layouts designed for grayscale reading.
Dark mode implementations that use the blue channel as the only accent.
detectorSignal: All CSS color declarations fall within the gray/hue-less range (saturation below 10%)
matcherProposal: Parse all CSS color declarations. Compute HSL saturation for each. Flag if 95%+ of colors have saturation <10%. Count distinct hue values - if fewer than 2 hues beyond gray appear, flag as monochrome default. Exclude images.
detectorLimitations: Cannot detect colors applied through images or SVGs.
Design systems may define accent colors that are not used in the current view.
Some accessibility high-contrast modes strip color intentionally.
falsePositiveScenarios: A legitimate monochrome brand identity (e.g., Apple, Spotify's neutral interfaces).
An interface designed for accessibility where color is intentionally minimized.
A focused reading mode where interface chrome is designed to be invisible.
mitigationStrategy: Flag only when the monochrome interface has interactive elements (buttons, links) that lack any color differentiator. Monochrome is acceptable for content-display interfaces.

fixturePlans.violation: A SaaS dashboard with gray sidebar, gray buttons, gray links, gray cards - no color anywhere except black text.
fixturePlans.control: Same dashboard with gray interface but blue accent on all links, buttons, and active navigation items.
fixturePlans.borderline: A photography portfolio with gray navigation, black text, and no accent color - the photos are the color.

overlapNotes: Opposite concern from color.purple-gradient-default - this targets no-color rather than overuse of one color.
